title: Provincial Nominee Program : Express Entry process Who can apply - Canada.ca
content:
To be eligible for the Provincial Nominee Program (PNP)
- You must be eligible for 1 of the province or territory’s PNP programs.
- The province or territory must nominate you.
- You must be eligible for 1 of the 3 Express Entry programs: Federal Skilled Worker Program Federal Skilled Trades Program Canadian Experience Class
- Federal Skilled Worker Program
- Federal Skilled Trades Program
- Canadian Experience Class
If a province or territory nominates you through a PNP stream, it will be listed on your nomination certificate. You can confirm this with the province or territory.
Before you apply through Express Entry, you must check the eligibility requirements for both of the following:
- the PNP program of the province or territory where you want to live and work
- the Express Entry program you want to apply for

## Find a province or territory for nomination
To be nominated by a province or territory, follow the instructions on their website and contact them directly.
Select a province or territory to learn about their PNP programs
- Newfoundland and Labrador
- Northwest Territories
- Prince Edward Island
Nunavut does not have a provincial nominee program. Learn more about how to immigrate to Nunavut .
Quebec does not have a provincial nominee program. Learn more about Quebec’s immigration programs

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
